hero.title: Image Carousel
hero.subtitle: Browse through images and discover the design strategies and optimizations behind each step.

basic.title: Navigate, jump, and loop
basic.description: The core behavior of an image carousel. Navigate with prev/next buttons, jump to a specific image via dots. Toggle the loop option to see the difference at boundaries.
basic.hint: Click buttons and dots, and toggle the loop option
basic.insight: The compound component pattern lets you freely rearrange Viewport, buttons, and dots. Context supplies state, so layout order doesn't affect behavior.

flux.title: One Reducer, many inputs
flux.description: Button clicks, dot clicks, keyboard arrows, autoplay timer — actions from different sources all converge into a single Reducer. Watch the action log in real time.
flux.hint: Alternate between buttons, dots, and keyboard while watching the log
flux.insight: Separating action sources from state mutation logic means adding a new input method never touches business logic.

autoplay.title: Autoplay meets manual control
autoplay.description: Slides advance automatically at a set interval. The moment the user interacts manually, the timer resets. Hovering pauses it.
autoplay.hint: Enable autoplay, then click a button to see the timer reset
autoplay.insight: Without resetting the timer on manual interaction, automatic transitions overlap with user actions, creating UX jank. Including currentIndex as a dependency solves this naturally.

performance.title: Preparing behind the scenes
performance.description: Loading 100 images at once would blow up the network. We detect user intent in 3 stages and preload only what's needed.
performance.hint: Hover to see hover state, slide to see active state
performance.insight: Airbnb detects user intent in 3 stages: idle → hover → active. On hover, preload 1 image. On actual slide, preload 3. This eliminates buffering entirely.

responsive.title: Resolution for every device
responsive.description: Pushing 4K originals to mobile is wasteful. The srcSet attribute lets the browser pick the right size for the device.
responsive.hint: Compare download sizes with and without srcSet
responsive.insight: object-fit: cover handles mismatched aspect ratios without layout breakage, and srcSet + sizes optimizes bandwidth. All achieved with HTML alone — no JavaScript needed.

keyboard.title: No mouse required
keyboard.description: Every interaction works with the keyboard alone. Arrow keys to navigate, Home/End to jump to the start or end.
keyboard.hint: Tab to focus, then use arrow keys and Home/End
keyboard.insight: tabIndex={0} makes the entire carousel focusable, and onKeyDown intercepts arrow keys to dispatch Reducer actions.

a11y.title: Screen readers see the carousel too
a11y.description: Follows the WAI-ARIA Carousel pattern. Proper roles and ARIA attributes ensure screen reader users get the same experience.
a11y.insight: When prefers-reduced-motion is detected, animations are disabled and autoplay stops. Accessibility is not a finishing touch — it must be designed in from the start.

attrs.regionRole: Identifies the carousel area as a landmark
attrs.carouselRoledescription: Tells screen readers the region's purpose is a carousel
attrs.ariaLive: Announces slide changes to screen readers (off during autoplay)
attrs.slideRoledescription: Specifies the group is a slide
attrs.tablist: Declares the dot indicator as tab navigation
attrs.tabSelected: Lets screen readers recognize the active slide

descriptions.images: Array of image data ({ src, alt, srcSet? })
descriptions.loop: Wrap around from last slide to first
